CREATE TABLE BPEL_Processes (id INT PRIMARY KEY, version INT, name VARCHAR(255));
CREATE TABLE Partner_Links (id INT PRIMARY KEY, process_id INT, FOREIGN KEY (process_id) REFERENCES BPEL_Processes(id));
CREATE TABLE Instances (id INT PRIMARY KEY, process_id INT, state VARCHAR(50), FOREIGN KEY (process_id) REFERENCES BPEL_Processes(id));
INSERT INTO BPEL_Processes (id, version, name) VALUES (5, 1, 'ProcessA'), (5, 2, 'ProcessA'), (6, 1, 'ProcessB');
INSERT INTO Instances (id, process_id, state) VALUES (1, 5, 'completed'), (2, 5, 'running');
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Instances WHERE process_id = 5 AND state = 'running';
SELECT * FROM Partner_Links WHERE process_id IN (SELECT id FROM BPEL_Processes WHERE name = 'ProcessA');
SELECT * FROM Instances WHERE process_id = (SELECT id FROM BPEL_Processes WHERE name = 'ProcessA' AND version = 1);
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Partner_Links WHERE process_id = 6;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Instances WHERE process_id = 5 AND state = 'completed';
